title: Volume quadlet ignoring options (most of the times)
body:
### Issue Description

I'm setting up multiple volumes using [volume units](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#volume-units-volume), the volume name seems to be honored always but other options like device and options are ignored most of the times.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Declare a container unit /etc/containers/systemd/alpine.container
```
[Unit]
Description=Example

[Container]
Image=docker.io/alpine:latest
Volume=logs.volume:/extralogs
Exec=sleep 5000

[Service]
Restart=always
TimeoutStartSec=900

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```
2. Declare a volume unit /etc/containers/systemd/logs.volume
```
[Volume]
VolumeName=logs
Options=bind
Device=/var/logs
```
3. reload the units `systemctl daemon-reload`
4. start alpine service `systemctl start alpine.service`

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman volume inspect logs
[
     {
          "Name": "logs",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/logs/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-04-12T23:47:27.117413397Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 6
     }
]
```
the volume was created but the options were ignored

### Describe the results you expected

```
$ podman volume inspect logs
[
     {
          "Name": "logs",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/logs/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-04-13T05:50:43.36266973Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {
               "device": "/var/logs",
               "o": "bind"
          },
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 5
     }
]
```
the options specified should be honor (Option and device)

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

```
$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="41.20250315.3.0 (CoreOS)"
```

i noticed a new minor version (5.4.2) is available but the patch notes do not mention anything in regard this issue.

### Additional information

The issue seems to be inconsistent, sometimes it does work fine, originally i thought only the first time when the image needs to be pull, but after  removing the image and volume and retry the issue persist, seems to be failing most of the times.

i also tried adding Requires and After values in the container [Unit] section with no difference.

I **believe** is some time of race condition, since looking into the logs i see the command 
`/usr/bin/podman volume create --ignore --opt device=/var/logs --opt o=bind logs` being executed and when run manually generate the correct volume.

i also see in the logs journalctl -b | grep "volume create"
 volume create logs
so a race condition issue makes sense to me, and since the second one (executed by the logs-volume.service) has --ignore nothings breaks and appear as everything worked but is creating/using the wrong volume

comments.body:
> The generated service is a one-time command that ensures that the volume exists on the host, creating it if needed.

As documented the volume is only created with the option if it did not already exist. Are you sure the volume did not existed before?

The thing about volumes is that if they are specified on podman run by name and do not exist then it is created with the default options. So if this is really a race then I would guess the alpine unit is started before the volume create unit was finished.

> Are you sure the volume did not existed before?

yes, i stop the service, remove the volume and then restart the services, **most of the times** it gets re-created without options and i see both create commands in the logs

I've tried to reproduce this several times with both `5.3.2` and `5.4.2` and in all cases the options were set correctly. I also tried both rootful and rootless and again all worked.

 I've noticed that the `MountCount` in your inspect is `0` while I would expect it to be `1`. So, I'm sorry for asking, but, are you sure you're looking at the correct volume?

Also, can you try with `5.4.2`? Maybe there was a hiccup that was fixed without us noticing.

i'm sure i'm looking at the right volume 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7633ce4-755e-4eec-86b0-effb2d072465)

It's odd, seems like the first time is done right, but subsequent ones are not (most of the time).

i can try the version 5.4.2 once it gets into fedora core os, but the patch notes for it doesn't seems to include a fix for this.

I don't know what to tell you. I just tried with Fedora Core 41 running podman 5.4.1 and still couldn't reproduce:
```
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.4.1
API Version:   5.4.1
Go Version:    go1.23.7
Git Commit:    b79bc8afe796cba51dd906270a7e1056ccdfcf9e
Built:         Tue Mar 11 00:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo systemctl stop alpine.service
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo systemctl stop logs-volume.service
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo podman volume rm logs
logs
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo podman volume list
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo systemctl start alpine.service
[core@ip-172-31-28-192 systemd]$ sudo podman volume inspect logs
[
     {
          "Name": "logs",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/logs/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-04-15T20:58:33.752054187Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {
               "device": "/var/logs",
               "o": "bind"
          },
          "MountCount": 1,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 0
     }
]
```

I don't know what can cause this

i think the problem is that the volume service is active (not stopped), after stopping it and trying all again it seems to work, so essentially 

1. start service container (volume created properly and volume service started)
2. stop service container (leaving volume service active)
3. Delete volume
4. Start service container (volume created without parameter)
I would expect the volume service to restart with it since the volume doesn't exist.

This is how systemd works. When service A depends on service B, starting A will start B, but stopping A will not stop B. Then, starting service A will not cause service B to start again as it is already running. Furthermore, since Quadlet volume units are a single call, they are defined as one-shot and therefore are not monitored in the same way that container services are. As a result, deleting the volume while the service is seen as running does not trigger a restart.

This why my sequence starts with stopping both services and deleting the volume.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
